model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size lets the MLP see substantially more unique training examples, which often improves validation loss in short-budget CPU training without adding architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["summaries","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current setup is training much better under the fixed-step budget, so the next low-complexity lever is a modest width increase to reduce underfitting without changing the overall MLP design or optimization recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gain so far came from using more examples per optimization step. Pushing batch size one notch further is a simple continuation of that trend and should still be tractable for this MLP on CPU.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The largest gains have come from exposing the model to more data within the fixed step budget. Trying one more batch-size increase is the highest-signal next move before changing the optimization recipe or architecture again.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After scaling batch size up aggressively, the most likely remaining mismatch is an undersized learning rate. A moderate LR increase is a simple way to better exploit the larger-batch regime without adding new mechanisms.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Optimization is working well in the large-batch regime, so a modest width increase is a simple next step to reduce remaining underfitting without changing the training loop or scheduler.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run is still improving from modest capacity increases, and this MLP remains relatively small. Increasing width again is the simplest high-probability way to reduce underfitting while preserving the now-effective optimizer, schedule, and large-batch setup.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-2","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"After several successful capacity and batch-size increases, the current setup is more likely over-regularized than under-optimized. Reducing weight decay is a simple way to let the larger MLP fit better within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The large-batch configuration is still improving, and the recent gains suggest optimization remains slightly conservative. A further moderate learning-rate increase is a simple next test with higher upside than another width change at this point.","secondary_modes":["micro","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most reliable source of improvement in this run has been increasing data exposure per optimization step. Trying one more batch-size increase is the cleanest way to test whether that trend still has headroom before changing the model or scheduler again.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The jump to batch size 2048 produced the biggest recent gain, which makes a follow-up learning-rate increase the most plausible low-complexity way to better match the new optimization regime without further increasing runtime.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last learning-rate increase overshot and degraded validation loss, so the highest-value next move is to revert to the previously better rate before exploring other axes.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, AdamW's default second-moment averaging can be too sluggish. Lowering beta2 is a compact way to make adaptation more responsive in this short-budget, very-large-batch regime.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.98)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering beta2 helped in the short-budget regime, which suggests the optimizer still benefits from faster second-moment adaptation. Testing one more step in that direction is a compact continuation of the best recent signal.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current cosine schedule never decays below 10% of peak LR. With only 256 steps and strong early optimization already in place, allowing the learning rate to anneal closer to zero may improve the final fit without changing the rest of the training recipe.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return 0.1 + 0.9 * 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","old":"        return 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The lower cosine floor hurt validation loss, so the next step should restore the previously better schedule shape before using the remaining budget on other directions.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Most gains have come from optimization and effective data throughput, which suggests the training setup is now strong enough to support a slightly deeper MLP. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The recent depth increase helped, which suggests the model is still somewhat capacity-limited under the current optimized training setup. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 28","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Capacity increases have been the strongest remaining source of verified gains once the optimizer and batch regime were tuned.
